candidates.why: Direct match for total current assets on the balance sheet.

candidates.why: Direct balance-sheet cash and cash equivalents line.
Looks like a component/residual rather than the primary reported total.
Restricted cash should not be used for CHE when a standard cash and cash equivalents line exists.

candidates.row_label: Cash and cash equivalents at beginning of period

candidates.row_label: Net increase/(decrease) in cash and cash equivalents
candidates.why: Flow, not stock.

candidates.row_label: Net exchange gain/loss on cash and cash equivalents
candidates.why: FX effect, not stock.

variables.notes: Selected the explicit total current liabilities row.

candidates.why: Appears in the current liabilities section and is the best available match for short-term debt; lease liabilities are listed separately and excluded.
Debt instrument, but not clearly identified as current portion and may be non-current in some periods.
Explicitly short-term but lease liabilities must be excluded from STD baseline.
Operating liability, not debt.
Too broad and includes non-debt items.

variables.notes: R:nc. Best semantic match is 'Interest -Bearing Liabilities' in the current-liabilities block, but the same label also appears in the non-current section, so the mapping is ambiguous from label alone.

candidates.why: Could include payroll taxes/social charges rather than income taxes payable; not a clean TXP match.
Deferred tax is non-current/non-payable tax accounting, not taxes payable.
Too broad and not specifically taxes payable.

variables.notes: No clear row for current taxes payable/current tax liabilities payable is present. 'Accrued Public Charges' is not specific enough to treat as TXP.

candidates.why: Direct PPE line and preferred as the reported net PPE total.
Gross PPE exists, but total/net PPE is preferred when available.
